JSONRPCError.description: A response to a request that indicates an error occurred. `id` is optional because Zed (and per JSON-RPC spec) sends error responses without an id when the error is for a notification (which has no id).

JSONRPCNotification.description: A notification which does not expect a response.

JSONRPCRequest.description: A request that expects a response.

JSONRPCResponse.description: A successful (non-error) response to a request.

RequestId.description:
A JSON-RPC request identifier — either a string or an integer.

`#[serde(untagged)]` is intentionally NOT used here. Untagged enum deserialization inside serde's internally-tagged `ClientRequest` enum (which uses `#[serde(tag = "method")]`) triggers a known serde limitation: the error from the first failed variant attempt ("invalid type: integer, expected a string") leaks out instead of being silently retried. A manual `Visitor` with `deserialize_any` avoids this.

description: Refers to any valid JSON-RPC object that can be decoded off the wire, or encoded to be sent.
title: JSONRPCMessage
